questions.hint: Shines in the sky during the day

questions.english: The literary text ___ of semantic layers
A philosopher should be ___ and contemplative
I like to read books in the library

questions.explanation: Use ذَهَبْتُ (first person) not ذَهَبَت (third person feminine)

questions.english: The Arabic language is one of the most beautiful languages in the world
questions.answer: اللُّغَةُ العَرَبِيَّةُ هِيَ مِن أَجْمَلِ لُغَاتِ العَالَم

questions.wrongSentence: الكِتَاب الذِي كَتَبَهُ الأَدِيب جَمِيلٌ جِدًا
questions.correctSentence: الكِتَابُ الَّذِي كَتَبَهُ الأَدِيبُ جَمِيلٌ جِدًا
questions.english: The book that the author wrote is very beautiful
questions.explanation: Use الَّذِي for relative pronoun referring to masculine singular

questions.english: She studies at the university every morning
questions.answer: هِيَ تَدْرُسُ فِي الجَامِعَةِ كُلَّ صَبَاح

questions.wrongSentence: إِن الطُّلَّاب الذِين اجْتَهَدُوا يَنَالُوا جَوَائِز
questions.correctSentence: إِنَّ الطُّلَّابَ الَّذِينَ اجْتَهَدُوا يَنَالُونَ جَوَائِزَ
questions.english: The students who worked hard will receive prizes
questions.explanation: إِنَّ requires accusative case; verb يَنَالُونَ (plural) not يَنَالُوا (jussive)

questions.english: The cultural heritage reflects the identity of peoples
Were it not for science, humanity would not have advanced
questions.explanation: لَوْلَا requires nominative; الإِنْسَانِيَّة is feminine so use تَقَدَّمَتِ

questions.english: The manuscripts preserved by scholars are a treasure for humanity
questions.answer: المَخْطُوطَاتُ الَّتِي حَفِظَهَا العُلَمَاءُ كَنْزٌ لِلبَشَرِيَّة

questions.wrongSentence: مَا مِن أُمَّةٌ ازْدَهَرَت إِلَّا وَقَد أَوْلَت الثَّقَافَة أَهَمِّيَّة قُصْوَى
questions.correctSentence: مَا مِن أُمَّةٍ ازْدَهَرَتْ إِلَّا وَقَدْ أَوْلَتِ الثَّقَافَةَ أَهَمِّيَّةً قُصْوَى
questions.english: No nation has flourished without giving culture utmost importance
questions.explanation: After مِن use tanwin jarr (ـٍ); proper tashkeel on verbs in connected speech

options.feedback: This is 'Thank you' - use it when thanked

questions.situation: Someone asks: مَا اسْمُك؟ (What is your name?)

options.feedback: This means 'I am fine' - for 'how are you?'
This asks 'Where are you from?' - a new question

questions.situation: You want to order water in a restaurant. What do you say?

options.feedback: Perfect! أُرِيد means 'I want', مِن فَضْلَك = please
This asks for the bill, not water

questions.situation: At a shop, the price is 50 dirhams. You think it's too expensive. What do you say?

options.text: هَذَا غَالٍ جِدًا، هَل يُمْكِن التَّخْفِيض؟

options.feedback: This talks about the weather, not the price
This says you want to go to the airport

questions.situation: Your friend looks sad. You ask: مَا الأَمْر؟ They respond: أَنَا مَرِيض. What do you say?

options.feedback: سَلَامَتَك is the perfect response to illness
This is a farewell, not appropriate here

questions.situation: In a business meeting: زَمِيلُك يَقُول: أَعْتَقِد أَنَّ خُطَّتَنَا تَحْتَاج إِلَى مُرَاجَعَة. (Your colleague says: I think our plan needs review.)

options.text: أَتَّفِق مَعَك، دَعْنَا نَنْظُر فِي التَّفَاصِيل

options.feedback: Too dismissive for a business context

questions.situation: Someone asks your opinion on a cultural topic: مَا رَأْيُك فِي الأَدَب العَرَبِي؟

options.text: الأَدَب العَرَبِي غَنِيٌّ جِدًا، أُحِب أَشْعَار المُتَنَبِّي
options.feedback: Rich, informed response showing cultural knowledge

questions.situation: During a debate: خَصْمُك يَقُول: التَّكْنُولُوجيَا دَمَّرَت التَّوَاصُل الإِنْسَانِي. كَيْف تَرُد؟

options.text: بَل عَزَّزَت التَّكْنُولُوجيَا التَّوَاصُل لِأَنَّهَا رَبَطَت الشُّعُوب عَبْرَ القَارَّات

questions.situation: مُحَاضِر يَسْأَل عَن الفَرْق بَيْن الفُصْحَى والعَامِّيَّة: مَا هُوَ دَوْر الفُصْحَى فِي العَالَم العَرَبِي الحَدِيث؟

options.text: الفُصْحَى هِيَ لُغَة التَّعْلِيم والأَدَب والإِعْلَام الرَّسْمِي، وَتُوَحِّد العَرَب بِثَقَافَة مُشْتَرَكَة

options.feedback: Inaccurate and dismissive of a rich tradition

questions.situation: In an advanced literary discussion: كَيْف تَرَى دَوْر الاسْتِعَارَة فِي الشِّعْر الجَاهِلِي مُقَارَنَةً بِالشِّعْر الحَدِيث؟

options.text: فِي الجَاهِلِيَّة كَانَت الاسْتِعَارَة مُرْتَبِطَة بِالطَّبِيعَة والحَرْب، بَيْنَمَا حَمَلَت فِي الحَدِيث أَبْعَادًا وُجُودِيَّة وَنَفْسِيَّة

questions.english: In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
questions.focusSound: Full tajweed: idgham, ikhfaa, ح sounds

questions.english: By time, indeed mankind is in loss (Quran 103:1-2)

questions.riddle: لَهُ أَوْرَاق وَلَيْس شَجَرَة، وَيُعَلِّمُك وَلَيْس مُعَلِّمًا. مَا هُوَ؟
questions.riddleEn: It has pages but is not a tree, and it teaches you but is not a teacher. What is it?

questions.riddle: أَكُون حَارًّا فِي النَّهَار وَأَغِيب فِي اللَّيْل. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: I am hot during the day and disappear at night. What am I?

questions.riddle: يَسَعُ كُلَّ شَيْء لَكِن لَا يَسَعُهُ شَيْء. مَا هُوَ؟
questions.riddleEn: It can hold everything but nothing can hold it. What is it?

questions.riddle: أَنَا بِلَا عَيْنَيْن وَمَع ذَلِك أَرَى المَاضِي وَالحَاضِر. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: I have no eyes yet I see the past and present. What am I?

questions.riddle: كُلَّمَا أَخَذْت مِنِّي أَزْدَاد. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: The more you take from me, the bigger I get. What am I?

questions.riddle: أَنَا مَوْجُود فِي دَقِيقَة لَكِن لَيْس فِي سَنَة. مَوْجُود فِي لَحْظَة لَكِن لَيْس فِي وَقْت. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: I exist in a minute but not in a year. In a moment but not in time. What am I?
questions.answerEn: The letter N (in minute/moment but not year/time)
questions.hint: Think about the Arabic letters in these words

questions.riddle: لِي رَأْس وَلَا أَعِيش، وَلِي ذَيْل وَلَا أَمِيل. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: I have a head but don't live, a tail but don't sway. What am I?

questions.riddle: أَنَا كَلِمَة مِن سِتَّة أَحْرُف. اقْلِبْنِي وَأَصْبَحْت اثْنَيْ عَشَر. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: I am a word of six letters. Flip me and I become twelve. What am I?
questions.answer: دَرْجَة (درجة → درجات is 12 steps metaphorically, or جذور/root games)
questions.hint: Think about numbers and Arabic word roots

questions.riddle: مَا الشَّيْء الَّذِي يَتَكَلَّم بِكُل اللُّغَات دُون أَن يَتَعَلَّمَهَا؟
questions.riddleEn: What speaks all languages without having learned them?

questions.riddle: أَنَا أَجْمَع وَلَا أُحْصَى، وَأَبْقَى وَلَا أُرَى، يَشْتَرِيني الفُقَرَاء وَيَبِيعُنِي الأَغْنِيَاء. مَا أَنَا؟
questions.riddleEn: I am collected yet uncountable, I remain but unseen, the poor buy me and the rich sell me. What am I?

questions.riddle: فِيَّ بِدَايَة كُل شَيْء وَنِهَايَة الزَّمَان، وَأَنَا مَوْجُود فِي المَكَان لَكِن لَيْس فِي أَيِّ وَقْت. مَا الحَرْف الَّذِي يَصِفُنِي?
questions.riddleEn: I am in the beginning of everything and the end of time, I exist in place but not in any time. What letter/concept am I?
questions.hint: A deep philosophical and linguistic riddle
questions.explanation: كُل شَيْء starts with ك, نِهَايَة الزَّمَان = ن in زمان, المَكَان has ك but الزَّمَن/وَقْت don't start with same letter — this tests meta-linguistic awareness
